claim: All AI coding abstractions (rules, commands, skills, agents, MCP) exist to manage context windows; keep context small and purposeful by using progressive disclosure and specialized agents
actionable: Organize code with agents.md for global rules, reference folders for on-demand context, and separate agents for different concerns to keep each context focused
source_title: AI Coding Agents Advanced Guide: Subagents, Skills, MCP, LSP, Commands, AGENTS.md, Hooks Explained

claim: AGENTS.md is a global rules file always loaded in context; include project description, tech stack, coding rules (e.g., use bun not npm), and post-feature requirements (docs, tests)
actionable: Create AGENTS.md with: project description, tech stack, build tools, code style rules, documentation standards, test requirements
source_title: AI Coding Agents Advanced Guide: Subagents, Skills, MCP, LSP, Commands, AGENTS.md, Hooks Explained

claim: Slash commands (/command) are user-initiated repeatable instructions; use them for things you manually trigger (like creating PRs) while skills are agent-initiated when agents recognize they need them
actionable: Create slash commands in .opencode/commands/ or .claude/commands/ for actions you explicitly request; reserve skills for agent-recognized needs
source_title: AI Coding Agents Advanced Guide: Subagents, Skills, MCP, LSP, Commands, AGENTS.md, Hooks Explained

claim: Skills are agent-initiated prompts with small descriptions always loaded; when agent detects it needs a skill, full instructions load dynamically to preserve context while extending capabilities
actionable: Create skills/ folders with separate skill.md files and helper scripts; include clear name/description so agents recognize when to invoke them
source_title: AI Coding Agents Advanced Guide: Subagents, Skills, MCP, LSP, Commands, AGENTS.md, Hooks Explained

claim: Primary agents are for interactive back-and-forth loops (you ask questions, wait, ask more); sub-agents are for delegated tasks with clear output (run tests, explore codebase, review code)
actionable: Create primary agents for ongoing conversation; spawn sub-agents for specific tasks to keep main context clean and prevent context pollution from long exploration
source_title: AI Coding Agents Advanced Guide: Subagents, Skills, MCP, LSP, Commands, AGENTS.md, Hooks Explained

claim: Define agent permissions granularly; specify which tools agents can access (write, edit, bash, add), which sub-agents they can spawn, and which skills they can use to prevent misuse
actionable: In agent configs, explicitly deny tools and skills by default; only allow what's needed (e.g., review agent gets read/bash but not write/add)
source_title: AI Coding Agents Advanced Guide: Subagents, Skills, MCP, LSP, Commands, AGENTS.md, Hooks Explained

claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers auto-populate many tools, often inflating context window unnecessarily; most MCPs like GitHub MCP add 40+ tools that clutter context; direct bash/CLI commands are often better
actionable: Use MCPs selectively (e.g., context7 for documentation); prefer bash commands over MCPs for GitHub operations unless the MCP is minimal
source_title: AI Coding Agents Advanced Guide: Subagents, Skills, MCP, LSP, Commands, AGENTS.md, Hooks Explained

claim: LSP (Language Server Protocol) enables agents to navigate codebases via go-to-definition, find symbols, and detect syntax errors instead of reading files manually
actionable: Configure LSP_SERVER_PATH env var to enable agents to use language server features for codebase navigation
source_title: AI Coding Agents Advanced Guide: Subagents, Skills, MCP, LSP, Commands, AGENTS.md, Hooks Explained

claim: Hooks let you run actions after events (after agent completes, before tool use); in Claude Code this is easy via settings.json, but OpenCode requires writing plugins
actionable: In Claude Code, define hooks in settings.json to trigger notifications, linting, or formatting after agent completion
source_title: AI Coding Agents Advanced Guide: Subagents, Skills, MCP, LSP, Commands, AGENTS.md, Hooks Explained

claim: Context rot is real: agents perform worse when context windows are filled; needle-in-haystack studies show performance degradation as context grows, especially with mixed information
actionable: Keep context windows lean; remove irrelevant information and use progressive disclosure to minimize irrelevant tokens
source_title: AI Coding Agents Advanced Guide: Subagents, Skills, MCP, LSP, Commands, AGENTS.md, Hooks Explained

claim: Always create unit tests for features and organize them in a tests/ folder; have agents run tests via sub-agents and delegate test fixing to specialized test runner sub-agents
actionable: Create tests/ folder structure; define test runner skill/sub-agent to run tests and fix failures with limited scope
source_title: AI Coding Agents Advanced Guide: Subagents, Skills, MCP, LSP, Commands, AGENTS.md, Hooks Explained
